items.title: The 'uncontrolled' police department behind fatal shooting of Perth girl

children.content: The grandfather of Australian schoolgirl Hania Ahmed recounts the aftermath of her fatal shooting in Pakistan, as a senior lawyer condemns police for employing a "deliberate policy of lethal force".

gallery.altText: Headshot of a father and his young daughter holding up a graduation certificate.
Selfie of a man hugging his young daughter and forming finger signs.
A grieving older man covers his eyes while seated on a grey sofa.

items.title: Wild birds tested for bird flu in Perth, Geraldton and South Australia

children.content: Two dead seabirds have been collected and are being tested for the H5N1 bird flu virus in South Australia, as well as another two wild birds in Western Australia, following Australia's first confirmed cases of the deadly variant this week.

image.altText: A hand with a cloth holding the beak of a dead grey and white bird on a beach.

items.title: Australia approves restricted use of herbicide banned in 70 other countries

children.content: The Australian Pesticides and Veterinary Medicines Authority concludes its nearly 30-year review of the controversial but popular herbicide.

image.altText: A label on a plastic tub reads CORROSIVE LIQUID, TOXIC N.O.S. PARAQUAT DICHLORIDE

images.altText: Harry Kane sinks to his knees and puts his head in his hands
Harry Kane sits on the grass and looks up at the ref to complain

articles.title: Ronaldo roars back to life, with his moments off the ball the most telling

children.content: Cristiano Ronaldo came into Portugal’s second World Cup group game under a firestorm of criticism. He answered with two goals, and important signs he is willing to be a team player.

image.altText: Cristiano Ronaldo smirks in the tunnel before the game

children.content: England is held to a frustrating 0-0 draw by a resolute Ghana after Cristiano Ronaldo scored twice in a 5-0 Portugal win over Uzbekistan. Follow live.

items.title: England frustrated by resolute Ghana, Ronaldo makes a statement

updates.title: FT: England held by resolute Ghana to 0-0 draw
HT: England held by ultra-defensive Ghana

items.title: 'I get a lot of anxiety': Borrowers caught out with high-interest car loans

children.content: A new report warns customers about traps they face when taking out a car loan, including high-interest rates and fees.

image.altText: A woman with a leg injury sits in the front seat of her car holding the steering wheel.

items.title: Australia launches world-first suicide prevention approach

children.content: Policymakers, mental health experts and those working on the front line will use the National Suicide Prevention Outcomes Map to gauge what services are working to prevent people from reaching critical crisis points.

image.altText: Jo Riley smiles while in a garden. She is wearing a colourful top and a necklace.

items.title: 'Punched in the guts': Sacked academics take uni to Fair Work Commission

children.content: Two senior academics from Macquarie University allege they were targeted for redundancy because of their union activity.

image.altText: A middle aged woman with short white hair sitting in a loungeroom

items.title: ASX to rise ahead of inflation figures, despite Wall Street's tech sell-off

children.content: The Australian share market is expected to rise despite a major sell-off across US tech, AI and semiconductor stocks, which resulted in steep losses on Wall Street. The ABS will release its May inflation data, which will be impacted by sharp falls in petrol and diesel prices. Follow live.

items.title: 'We've been looked after': Hantavirus cruise passengers out of quarantine

children.content: Six passengers from Australia and NZ linked to the deadly MV Hondius hantavirus outbreak are on their way home after completing 42 days of quarantine in Perth and being cleared to leave.

image.altText: A van carries people in the dark

gallery.altText: A group of people in full body protective gear are in a minibus

thumbnail.altText: A group of people in full body protective gear are in a minibus

items.title: Gardening Australia star discovered plants unknown to science but couldn't be his true self

children.content: Gardening Australia star Jerry Coleby-Williams hid his identity on TV for 26 years, but has never been ashamed of being a gay man.

image.altText: Jerry Coleby-Williams stands outside smiling, holding up a hanging pot overflowing with cherry tomatoes

items.title: Quiz: Join us for a mid-week trip around the world

children.content: Which town would you be in if you were sunbathing at Cable Beach? Where does Maldon sea salt come from?

items.title: Animals 'disappearing' from the wild as more people seek exotic pets

children.content: Australian and international authorities are "struggling" to keep up with regulating and policing the exotic pet market as demand surges.

items.title: Injury mars Australia's crushing T20 World Cup win over Pakistan

children.content: Australia cruises to a comfortable 113-run victory over Pakistan at Headingley, but there may be concern over a finger injury to one of their stars.

image.altText: Beth Mooney receives treatment during Australia's Women's T20 World Cup match against Pakistan.

items.title: Aussies produce mixed results in Wimbledon qualifying

children.content: Emerson Jones and Storm Hunter advance to the second round of Wimbledon's qualifying stage, but some of their fellow Australians are not as fortunate.

image.altText: Emerson Jones serves during the junior tournament at Wimbledon in 2025.

items.title: 'Look at us go!': First Nations actors celebrate Logies nods

children.content: With strong representation of Indigenous talent in this year's Logie Award nominations, actor Lila McGuire says it's a "very important time" and "a gift for young mob seeing so many of us excelling".

image.altText: LIla as Evonne takes a swing at a tennise ball during a match.

items.title: Fourth WA council in turmoil after three councillors quit in three days

children.content: Three councillors have quit the City of South Perth in three days, citing concerns over a rates hike and broader dysfunction within the council.

items.title: Angus Taylor refuses to commit to a multicultural Australia

children.content: The opposition leader, pressed multiple times on whether his party still supported multiculturalism or endorsed One Nation leader Pauline Hanson's vision of a "monoculture", refused to commit to either.

items.title: 'Ridiculous and disrespectful': Inquiry into NDIS cuts extended

children.content: Disability advocates welcome an extension to a Senate inquiry probing a bill paving the way for the biggest-ever cuts to the NDIS.

image.altText: A middle-aged white man sitting in a wheelchair. He is wearing glasses and a dark and scarf beanie

items.title: 'No longer appropriate': WA MP calls to end Lord's Prayer in parliament

children.content: A Labor MP says he wants the Lord’s Prayer scrapped from WA parliament, despite the reading of the Christian prayer being a long-held tradition before sessions.

items.title: Is it possible to have a monoculture? This WA town doesn't think so

children.content: A Muslim leader from a regional WA town says he would like to have a conversation with Pauline Hanson about her ideology following her comments calling for monocultural Australia.

items.title: Scientists 'blown away' by baby coral resilience on Ningaloo Reef

children.content: After suffering its worst bleaching event on record, only to be battered by Cyclone Narelle six months later, the World Heritage-listed reef is showing its first signs of recovery thanks to the efforts of marine scientists.

gallery.altText: Marine researchers goes underwater to place spawning cones to gather and preserve coral reefs.
Spawn cones deployed in the Bundegi Reef to collect baby corals in a bid to restore bleached corals in Ningaloo Reef.
Marine researchers and scientists from various organisations working at the Mesa Reef in Exmouth to restore the bleched corals

thumbnail.altText: Marine researchers goes underwater to place spawning cones to gather and preserve coral reefs.
Spawn cones deployed in the Bundegi Reef to collect baby corals in a bid to restore bleached corals in Ningaloo Reef.
